question: What ASME code classification is the NuScale containment vessel (CNV) designed to?

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure boundary to prevent the release of fission products and provides structural support for various reactor components. Materials used for the RPV are selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant's design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0008.title: Compliance with Codes and Code Cases
nuscale_ch05_0008.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Power Module (NPM) and its reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components comply with relevant U.S. regulations, specifically 10 CFR 50.55a and associated General Design Criteria. It details the use of ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) 2013 Edition for design, fabrication, testing, and classification of components, ensuring adherence to the highest quality standards and regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, specifying the use of nickel-based Alloy 718 for compatibility and stress corrosion cracking mitigation. It also outlines that preservice and inservice inspections and testing of pressure-retaining components are conducted according to the latest ASME BPVC Section XI requirements, as mandated by federal regulations, with inspection intervals and code editions specified in the inspection program.

nuscale_ch05_0007.title: Integrity of Reactor Coolant Boundary
nuscale_ch05_0007.summary: This section describes the design features of each NuScale Power Module (NPM) that ensure the integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) throughout the plant’s design life, in accordance with regulatory definitions. It details the components and systems included in the RCPB, such as the reactor pressure vessel, containment isolation valves, safety valves, and associated piping, emphasizing their roles in maintaining pressure containment and system safety.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located within the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. The RCS removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity during both normal and emergency operations.

edges.description: Section A describes the containment vessel (CNV) as surrounding the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B specifies that the RPV and pressure retaining components are designed to ASME BPVC Section III, Class 1, but does not explicitly mention the CNV, connecting them through their discussion of vessel classification and code compliance.

Section B specifies that threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, including those associated with the RPV and related components, conform to ASME BPVC Sections II and III, which provides a regulatory context that may also apply to the CNV as described in Section A.

Section A describes the physical arrangement of the containment vessel (CNV) relative to the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B defines the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) and its components, establishing that the CNV surrounds but is not part of the RCPB, which is relevant for determining the applicable ASME code classification.

Section A establishes that the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and associated pressure-retaining components are designed to ASME BPVC Section III Class 1, while Section B specifies that the threaded fasteners used in these components also conform to ASME BPVC Sections II and III, thus providing detailed material and design compliance information supporting Section A's general compliance statement.

Section A specifies that the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) components, which include the containment vessel as described in Section B, are designed to ASME BPVC Section III, Class 1, thus directly addressing the code classification for the CNV.

Section A specifies that pressure-retaining components associated with the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which includes the containment vessel (CNV), are designed as ASME Class 1, while Section B describes the system functions and components that are subject to these design requirements.

Section A specifies that threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary conform to ASME BPVC Sections II and III, while Section B defines the reactor coolant pressure boundary components, thereby connecting the ASME code requirements in A to the boundary components described in B.

Section A specifies that threaded fasteners in the RCS conform to ASME BPVC Sections II and III, while Section B describes the RCS and its pressure-retaining portions as part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, connecting the design requirements in A to the system described in B.

Section A defines the scope and integrity requirements of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB), while Section B describes how the pressure-retaining portions of the reactor coolant system (RCS), which include the containment vessel (CNV), contribute to maintaining the RCPB's integrity, thus connecting the system description to the regulatory boundary definition.
